name: Ocotea Odorifera
summary: Ocotea odorifera contains Safrole and is linked here to TRP channel modulation.
description: Ocotea odorifera contains Safrole and is linked here to TRP channel modulation.
contraindications: any internal use of concentrated extract or essential oil because its high safrole content is classified as a rodent hepatocarcinogen and was banned from human food use in the United States since 1960
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to safrole's carcinogenic profile and lack of an established safe exposure threshold
young children due to the same safrole carcinogenicity and hepatotoxicity caution
known allergy to Lauraceae family plants because Ocotea odorifera is in the laurel family

sources.title: Confirmation of ethnopharmacological anti-inflammatory properties of Ocotea odorifera and determination of its main active compounds
Identification of phenolic compounds and biologically related activities from Ocotea odorifera aqueous extract leaves
Exploring Contact Toxicity of Essential Oils against Sitophilus zeamais through a Meta-Analysis Approach
Comprehensive toxicity study of safrole using a medium-term animal model with gpt delta rats

dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Aromatic herb/spice forms and essential oils differ greatly and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
typical_dosage: Preparation-specific; use standardized product label for Aromatic herb/spice forms and essential oils differ greatly and require source-backed dosage review before therapeutic claims.
